lesson_name: Connection Patterns: EXEC Chains, DATA Wiring, Branch Paths
description: Teaches the model correct connection patterns at scale. Focus on EXEC chain linearity, DATA type matching, Branch completeness, and common wiring mistakes to avoid. These are the building blocks that must be perfect for large Blueprints to work.

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on BeginPlay prints Step 1, then Step 2, then Step 3, then Step 4, then Step 5 in sequence
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FiveSteps
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Step 1"]
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Step 2"]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Step 3"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Step 4"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Step 5"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on overlap sets IsActive to true, sets Score to 0, sets Health to 100, prints Game Started, and destroys itself, all in order
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_InitAndDestroy
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsActive: Bool = false
VAR Score: Int = 0
VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: SetVar [Variable=IsActive, Value=true]
NODE n3: SetVar [Variable=Score, Value=0]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health, Value=100.0]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Game Started"]
NODE n6: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with an IsAlive boolean. On overlap, check IsAlive. If true, print Still Alive and subtract 10 from Health. If false, print Already Dead
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_AliveCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsAlive: Bool = true
VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsAlive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Still Alive"]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n6: SubtractFloat [B=10.0]
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Already Dead"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Value [Float]

prompts.instruction: Create a pickup with a Value integer. On overlap, if Value is greater than 50, print Rare Item. If Value is less than or equal to 50, print Common Item. Then destroy in both cases
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RarityPickup
PARENT: Actor

VAR Value: Int = 25

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Value]
NODE n3: GreaterThan [B=50]
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Rare Item!"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Common Item"]
NODE n7: DestroyActor
NODE n8: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n4.False -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on BeginPlay gets Health, subtracts Damage from it, stores the result back in Health, then checks if Health is less than or equal to zero, and if so prints Dead
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageCalc
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0
VAR Damage: Float = 25.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Damage]
NODE n4: SubtractFloat
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n6: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Dead"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with Score and Multiplier variables. On a custom event AddPoints, get Score, add Multiplier times 10 to it, set Score back, and print the new Score
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreCalc
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0
VAR Multiplier: Int = 1

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="AddPoints"]
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Multiplier]
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat [B=10]
NODE n5: AddFloat
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n7: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n3.Value -> n4.A [Int]
DATA n2.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.B [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on overlap gets the distance to the overlapping actor. Use that same distance value in a print statement AND in a comparison to check if it's less than 500
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DistanceFanOut
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetDistanceTo
NODE n3: PrintString
NODE n4: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Close!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.InString [String]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with a Health variable. On AnyDamage, get Health, subtract the damage, store it in Health, use the SAME subtracted result to check if below zero AND to print the remaining health
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageFanOut
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Dead!"]
NODE n9: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n6.A [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on BeginPlay starts a looping timer called Pulse every 2 seconds. The Pulse custom event increments a Counter variable and prints the count
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimerPulse
PARENT: Actor

VAR Counter: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Pulse", Time=2.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Pulse"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=Counter]
NODE n5: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Counter]
NODE n7: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that starts a 1 second looping timer called Tick on BeginPlay. The Tick event subtracts 1 from TimeLeft. When TimeLeft reaches zero, print Times Up, clear the timer, and destroy
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Countdown
PARENT: Actor

VAR TimeLeft: Int = 10

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Tick", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Tick"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=TimeLeft]
NODE n5: SubtractFloat [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=TimeLeft]
NODE n7: LessThanOrEqual [B=0]
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Times Up!"]
NODE n10: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Tick"]
NODE n11: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Int]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with two separate event chains: BeginOverlap prints Entered and sets IsInside to true. EndOverlap prints Exited and sets IsInside to false. Both chains are independent
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TwoEvents
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsInside: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Entered"]
NODE n3: SetVar [Variable=IsInside, Value=true]
NODE n4: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Exited"]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=IsInside, Value=false]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with three independent event chains: BeginPlay starts a timer. The timer custom event increments a counter. AnyDamage subtracts health and checks for death. All three run independently
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ThreeChains
PARENT: Actor

VAR Counter: Int = 0
VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="Count", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Count"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=Counter]
NODE n5: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Counter]
NODE n7: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n8: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n9: SubtractFloat
NODE n10: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n11: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n12: Branch
NODE n13: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n12.Execute
EXEC n12.True -> n13.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n8.Value -> n9.A [Float]
DATA n7.Damage -> n9.B [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.Value [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n11.A [Float]
DATA n11.ReturnValue -> n12.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a switch that uses FlipFlop on overlap. First touch sets IsOn to true and prints Activated. Second touch sets IsOn to false and prints Deactivated. Alternates forever
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ToggleSwitch
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsOn: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: FlipFlop
NODE n3: SetVar [Variable=IsOn, Value=true]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Activated"]
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=IsOn, Value=false]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Deactivated"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create a treasure chest that on overlap uses DoOnce. The first time it prints You found gold and adds 50 to Gold. Any subsequent overlap does nothing
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_OneTimeChest
PARENT: Actor

VAR Gold: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: DoOnce
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Gold]
NODE n4: AddFloat [B=50]
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=Gold]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="You found gold!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Completed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

DATA n3.Value -> n4.A [Int]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Int]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on overlap checks if HasKey is true. If yes, check if HasBossKey is also true. If both are true, print Final Door Opens. If HasKey but not HasBossKey, print Need Boss Key. If no HasKey, print Need Key
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_NestedCheck
PARENT: Actor

VAR HasKey: Bool = false
VAR HasBossKey: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=HasKey]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=HasBossKey]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Final Door Opens!"]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Need Boss Key"]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Need Key"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n5.False -> n7.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a damage handler that checks damage amount. If over 50, print Critical Hit. If between 20 and 50, print Normal Hit. If under 20, print Weak Hit
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageTiers
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GreaterThan [B=50.0]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Critical Hit!"]
NODE n5: GreaterThan [B=20.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Normal Hit"]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Weak Hit"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n6.False -> n8.Execute

DATA n1.Damage -> n2.A [Float]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n1.Damage -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor with a Gate. BeginPlay opens the gate. A timer event tries to pass through the gate every second. When it passes, it prints Tick. On EndOverlap, close the gate so ticks stop
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateTimer
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="TryTick", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n4: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="TryTick"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Tick"]
NODE n6: Event_ActorEndOverlap

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n2.Close

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on overlap prints 3, waits 1 second, prints 2, waits 1 second, prints 1, waits 1 second, prints Go!, then destroys itself
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Countdown321
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="3"]
NODE n3: Delay [Duration=1.0]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="2"]
NODE n5: Delay [Duration=1.0]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="1"]
NODE n7: Delay [Duration=1.0]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Go!"]
NODE n9: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Completed -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Completed -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Completed -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on BeginPlay spawns a BP_Projectile at its own location, then prints Spawned
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Spawner
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: SpawnActorFromClass [ActorClass=BP_Projectile]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Spawned"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.SpawnTransform [Vector]

prompts.instruction: Create an actor that on overlap generates a random integer between 1 and 100, clamps it between 10 and 90, stores it in a Value variable, and prints the clamped value
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_RandomClamped
PARENT: Actor

VAR Value: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: RandomIntegerInRange [Min=1, Max=100]
NODE n3: Clamp [Min=10, Max=90]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Value]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
